panels.description: Number of sampled running-job observations in the selected time range. This is the evidence base for the dashboard and counts samples, not distinct jobs.
panels.title: Evidence Samples

panels.description: Share of sampled running-job observations requesting more than 64 CPU cores. A high value suggests demand for larger CPU nodes or more high-core-count partitions.
panels.title: Large CPU Demand

panels.description: Share of sampled running-job observations requesting more than 256 GiB of memory. This is a direct signal for high-memory node purchasing.
panels.title: High Memory Demand

panels.description: Share of sampled running-job observations requesting more than 48 hours of walltime. This helps distinguish short throughput workloads from long reservation-style workloads.
panels.title: Long Runtime Demand

panels.description: Share of sampled running-job observations requesting more than one node. A high value points toward interconnect, topology, and multi-node scheduling pressure.
panels.title: Multi-Node Demand

panels.description: Number of sampled running-job observations with requested GPU data in the selected range. Use the GPU heatmap below to see whether demand is mostly 1 GPU or multi-GPU.
panels.title: GPU Demand Samples

panels.description: Requested CPU-core distribution summarized over time. P50 approximates the typical request; P95 shows the high-end tail that can drive hardware sizing.
panels.title: CPU Typical vs Tail

panels.description: Requested memory distribution summarized over time. The distance between P50 and P95 shows whether memory demand is broad or dominated by a heavy tail.
panels.title: Memory Typical vs Tail

panels.description: Requested walltime distribution summarized over time. Use this to see whether users mostly ask for short jobs or long-running allocations.
panels.title: Walltime Typical vs Tail

panels.description: High-end requested MPI, node-count, and GPU trends. This is a compact view of topology and accelerator demand that may affect node type, interconnect, and GPU-density decisions.
panels.title: Topology And Accelerator Tail

panels.description: Heatmap of requested CPU-core distribution over time. Each row is a cumulative bucket and color intensity represents sampled observations in the query interval.
panels.title: CPU Distribution Heatmap

panels.description: Heatmap of requested memory distribution over time. Color intensity shows sampled observations per cumulative memory bucket.
panels.title: Memory Distribution Heatmap

panels.description: Heatmap of requested walltime distribution over time. It shows how requested run lengths shift across the selected period.
panels.title: Walltime Distribution Heatmap

panels.description: Heatmap of requested MPI process distribution over time. Useful for spotting shifts between serial, small parallel, and large parallel workloads.
panels.title: MPI Distribution Heatmap

panels.description: Heatmap of requested node-count distribution over time. It highlights single-node versus multi-node workload shape.
panels.title: Node Count Distribution Heatmap

panels.description: Heatmap of requested GPU-device distribution over time. It makes accelerator-heavy samples easy to distinguish from CPU-only jobs.
panels.title: GPU Distribution Heatmap

panels.description: Cumulative share of sampled running-job observations below each CPU bucket over the selected range. Read this as percentage of samples at or below each core count.
panels.title: CPU Cumulative Share

panels.description: Cumulative share of sampled running-job observations below each memory bucket over the selected range.
panels.title: Memory Cumulative Share

panels.description: Cumulative share of sampled running-job observations below each walltime bucket over the selected range.
panels.title: Walltime Cumulative Share

panels.description: Cumulative share of sampled running-job observations below each MPI-process bucket. Use this to understand whether parallel demand is mostly small, medium, or very large.
panels.title: MPI Cumulative Share

panels.description: Cumulative share of sampled running-job observations below each node-count bucket. This makes single-node versus multi-node demand visible for hardware and network planning.
panels.title: Node Count Cumulative Share

panels.description: Cumulative share of sampled running-job observations below each GPU bucket. Read this as the shape of explicit GPU requests, especially 1-GPU versus multi-GPU demand.
panels.title: GPU Cumulative Share
